name: Toby
observedShift: Toby has strong execution and team trust, but after being passed over last cycle he has become cautious about taking visible strategic positions. He keeps shipping, yet he is not consistently converting design leadership into org-level influence. I need this conversation to clarify whether the gap is confidence, sponsorship, or unclear expectations at the next level. He should leave with a concrete growth contract tied to decision ownership and cross-functional impact, not a generic development list.

focusPoints.label: Turning design leadership into org-level influence
focusPoints.reason: Whether he’s translating his execution and team trust into sponsorship and hard-to-ignore stakeholder relationships at the org level.

focusPoints.label: Decision ownership at the next level
focusPoints.reason: What “good” looks like for a Lead in this growth plan is owning the decisions, not just driving the work—so you’ll want to get specific about which decisions he will be accountable for and how quickly he can unblock them.

focusPoints.label: Cross-functional impact beyond the design team
focusPoints.reason: Whether he’s getting enough clarity and alignment on who the decision-makers are across Product/Engineering and how design’s recommendations become tradeoffs that other teams actually adopt.

focusPoints.label: What he’d want more of from you
focusPoints.reason: If the growth contract is about decision ownership and cross-functional impact, you’ll want to name the specific sponsorship, introductions, and air cover he needs from you to take visible strategic positions without getting stuck in caution.

issues: openingQuestion may lack forward developmental framing for Growth & career plan
